title: Canvas Items, Tags, Layers, Rendering, and Redraw · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss decisions for canvas items, tags, layers, rendering, and redraw and accepts only runnable changed-case evidence, explicit limitations, and named browser, Tcl, Tk, display, input, assistive-technology, file, package, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for tagged layered Canvas render plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence de…
objectives: Distinguish retained Canvas items, integer IDs, tags, configuration, coordinates, stacking order, and deletion.
Define wall, terrain, solution, focus, annotation, and status layers with stable stacking rules.
Use semantic tags for groups and stable model keys rather than retaining fragile positional ID lists.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-maze-solver-python cumulative accessible desktop maze portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python grid, graph, search, state, and scheduler models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts,
focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, display scaling,
packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazePolicy:
    rows: int = 12
    columns: int = 16
    seed: int = 314159
    maximum_cells: int = 16_384
    reduced_motion: bool = False


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class MazeEvidence:
    revision: str
    topology_digest: str
    action_trace_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str
    artifact_digest: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python maze solver portfolio ready"

steps.title: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Render Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz puzzle team handles case 5814 during a low-vision player session. Build deterministic Python evidence to reconcile retained render items, semantic tags, layer order, and redraw cleanup; native Tk behavior must not be inferred from browser execution; then inject one invalid wall, stale callback, or corrupted field and reject it. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real Tcl, Tk, tkinter, windows, Canvas, fonts, focus, input devices, assistive technology, files, platform timing, installers, and packaged desktop behavior require explicit controlled native transfer gates. Competency probe: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.

Evidence target: Maze solving, Tkinter, and Python Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Render Evidence.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify item counts, coordinates, tags, layer order, changed topology, and native screenshot or inspection evidence separately.
